spec_match.rationale: Uses anchor-name, position-anchor, @position-try, `position-try-options`, `container-type: anchored`, and real `@container anchored(--try-name)` rules that style the fallback badge for the chosen placement.

interactive.rationale: Five anchor buttons, width/height sliders that live-resize the scroll container, fallback-badge toggle. Eight controls — real interactivity.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises 'five anchor buttons positioned at all edges and the center, each with a CSS-anchor-positioned tooltip using anchor-name, @position-try fallback blocks, and position-try-order' — page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: The page feature-detects both anchor positioning and `container-type: anchored`. In supporting browsers, badge labels are driven by `@container anchored(--try-name)` instead of overflow inference. The JS inference remains only for fallback mode and `position-try-order: most-block-size` is still not isolated as its own comparison.

references.rationale: See-also links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5177580990496768 and the CSS Anchor Position L2 anchored container query section.

design_system.rationale: Variables-only colour palette; no raw hex in the script body.

openQuestions.title: Show the position-try-order: most-block-size effect explicitly
openQuestions.detail: Blurb mentions `position-try-order: most-block-size` but the demo never highlights when that ordering changes the picked fallback. A toggle that compares with/without the order keyword on the same anchor would teach the ordering effect.

summary: The tooltip demo now exercises the v143 anchored-container-query feature directly: tooltips are anchored containers, badge labels are styled by `@container anchored(--try-name)`, and fallback mode is clearly labelled when browser support is missing.
